reforms.description: The Austrian government announced an employment-creating package in January 1982, which includes an increase in allowances for research and development activities, aimed at boosting investment through various construction projects and medium-term structural adjustments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in R&D allowances is expected to stimulate investment and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The package was announced and implemented in 1982.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances funding for R&D activities, which is a critical aspect of innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: a second employment programme in September 1982, comprising not only short-term measures to stimulate construction and limit unemployment during the winter but also medium-term measures to promote structural adjustment.

reforms.description: To support residential construction, the tax allowance for savings for building purposes was increased from 10 to 13 percent.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the tax allowance encourages savings for building, which can lead to more residential construction and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax allowance increase was implemented in 1982.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a minor adjustment to existing tax policy aimed at stimulating residential construction.
reforms.source_quote: To raise the inflow of funds and support residential construction from January 1982 onwards, the tax allowance for savings for building purposes was increased from 10 to 13 per cent.

reforms.description: The participation fund law was enacted to allow shares of specialized participation funds to be deducted from taxes, making them more attractive to high tax rate payers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This law incentivizes investment in startups by making participation funds more attractive, potentially leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted and implemented in 1982.
reforms.importance_rationale: While beneficial, this reform is a minor adjustment to tax policy aimed at enhancing investment attractiveness.
reforms.source_quote: To improve the situation, the participation fund law (Beteiligungsfondsgesetz) was voted in March 1982.

reforms.description: The 'Top-Aktion' interest subsidy scheme for innovative investment was introduced to provide financial support for selected investment projects, aiming to guide restructuring towards positive adjustment incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme supports innovative investments, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was introduced and implemented in 1981.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme provides targeted support but is not a major overhaul of the innovation funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Top-Aktion', a new interest subsidy scheme for innovative investment, which came into effect in July 1981, with its strict selection criteria of investment projects may be seen as a guideline for the restructuring of the various instruments.
